description: Extend the POST/upsert backend-to-handler result contract so Slice 3 authorization outcomes are explicit instead of flowing through string-only not-authorized results, unknown failures, or exceptions.
acceptance-criteria: Upsert results model relationship-not-authorized with the external RelationshipAuthorizationFailure DTO, proposed value-source metadata, and transitional error messages.
Upsert results model security-configuration failure separately from 403 authorization denial.

Upsert results model temporary not-implemented/fail-closed staging for known-but-not-enabled strategies, with reason metadata that can distinguish strategy-not-enabled from existing-resource POST-as-update staging.

The POST handler maps relationship-not-authorized to 403, known-but-not-enabled/existing-resource staging to 501, and security-configuration failure to the canonical authorization configuration 500 response.
Existing POST success, reference failure, validation failure, identity conflict, ETag mismatch, profile data policy, and unknown-failure mappings remain unchanged.
The result contract does not expose Backend.Plans types across the Core.External boundary.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs for explicit Slice 3 result variants.
Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs for one-to-one handler mappings for the new result variants.

description: Add POST operation-neutral relationship authorization planning after route/resource/action and token context are known, and before request-dependent validation, reference resolution, or target identity work in the relational document store repository.
acceptance-criteria: Relational POST adapts AuthorizationStrategyEvaluator inputs to configured strategy metadata and calls proposed-value relationship planning once for the route/resource/action and token context.
NoAuthorizationRequired and NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired continue through the normal write path without relationship checks.

Security-configuration failures from planning return the new upsert security-configuration result before request-dependent POST validation, reference resolution, descriptor resolution, target lookup, or write executor execution, and win over known-but-not-enabled staging when both are present.

Any effective NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, People relationship, or custom view-based known-but-not-enabled strategy returns the temporary not-implemented result before request-dependent POST validation, reference resolution, descriptor resolution, target lookup, or write executor execution, whether it appears alone or mixed with supported EdOrg strategies.

Empty normalized EdOrg claims for supported EdOrg relationship POST return relationship-not-authorized/403 with no-claims metadata before request-dependent POST validation, reference resolution, descriptor resolution, target lookup, or write executor execution.
The preflight keeps NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired as a no-op and does not partially authorize when unsupported known strategies are also configured.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs for POST planning order before ExecuteWriteGuardRails invokes request-dependent work.

Review unit tests proving request-dependent validators, reference resolver, descriptor resolver, target lookup service, and write executor are not called for security-configuration, known-but-not-enabled, and empty-claims short-circuits.

description: Build the runtime extractor that reads proposed EdOrg authorization subjects from the finalized root RowBuffer produced by the write path.
acceptance-criteria: Proposed authorization values are read by RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueBinding.BindingIndex from the finalized root RootWriteRowBuffer, not from raw JSON.
Writable profile POST uses the effective ProfileAppliedWriteRequest.WritableRequestBody and any projected ProfileAppliedWriteContext before flattening and extraction.

Extraction occurs after reference resolution, descriptor resolution, key-unification/precomputed binding population, write-shape filtering, and application-computed defaults that are present in the row buffer.
Database-generated defaults are not treated as a source for authorization-required proposed subjects.
Missing, unresolved, or SQL-null proposed EdOrg subject values produce relationship-not-authorized metadata with ProposedValueMissing/element-required semantics and do not compose authorization SQL.
Multiple subjects within one relationship strategy remain AND semantics and multiple strategies remain OR semantics in the extracted runtime check input.

Extraction preserves configured strategy indexes, relationship-local order, subject ordinals, readable securable names, JSON paths, root table/column bindings, auth object metadata, and normalized EdOrg claim context for Slice 6.
steps-to-verify: Review the extractor implementation near the relational write executor or supporting backend helper for row-buffer-based value access.
Review unit tests where raw request JSON differs from the profile-applied writable body and authorization values come from the finalized row buffer.
Review unit tests for missing/null proposed values returning relationship element-required metadata before SQL execution.

description: Extend relationship authorization SQL generation so proposed-value EdOrg-only checks can be composed with create persistence for PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
acceptance-criteria: The compiler accepts RelationshipAuthorizationCheckTarget.Proposed and rejects mixed stored/proposed check batches for Slice 3.
Proposed EdOrg values are bound as SQL parameters allocated without collisions against claim EdOrg parameters, document insert parameters, and root insert parameters.
Token EdOrg IDs are always parameterized through the existing ClaimEducationOrganizationIds contract and are never inlined into SQL.
Normal RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly uses token claims against SourceEducationOrganizationId and proposed values against TargetEducationOrganizationId.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted swaps SourceEducationOrganizationId and TargetEducationOrganizationId filtering for proposed values.
Multiple strategies OR together, duplicate configured strategies remain distinct, and multiple proposed subjects within one strategy AND together.
Failed proposed OR groups throw the shared versioned AUTH1 failure-set payload with emitted check index, strategy ordinal, subject ordinal, and compact failure-kind codes only.
If any OR strategy succeeds, failures from other strategies are not surfaced.
PostgreSQL uses the established dms.throw_error AUTH1 mechanism and SQL Server uses the established CAST('AUTH1 - ...' AS INT) abort pattern.

Generated operation-specific proposed authorization SQL is not cached; only reusable operation-neutral metadata may be cached by the existing effective schema, mapping set, resource, strategy, and securable element boundaries.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler.cs or its replacement for proposed-target SQL generation.
Review SQL golden/assertion tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server normal, inverted, OR, AND, duplicate strategy, and parameter collision cases.
Review AUTH1 payload parser tests for proposed-value failure-kind codes and malformed payload handling.

description: Integrate proposed-value relationship authorization into the POST create-new write executor path after flattening and before create persistence.
acceptance-criteria: POST create-new evaluates proposed-value relationship authorization after reference and descriptor resolution and after the target is resolved as create-new.

Unauthorized proposed values return the new relationship-not-authorized upsert result before dms.Document insert, root insert, child inserts, referential identity maintenance, update-tracking/journal writes, or committed-response readback.
Reference-resolution failures remain reference-resolution results and are not converted into authorization denials unless the earlier empty-claims short-circuit already stopped the request.
Security-configuration and known-but-not-enabled planning failures remain operation-neutral preflight outcomes and do not run executor authorization.
The create-new authorization check and dms.Document insert execute in the same provider command/batch inside the request-scoped write transaction, with AUTH1 before the document insert.
Authorization failure rolls back the write session and returns the mapped relationship failure DTO without running a post-failure database lookup.
Authorized create-new continues through existing merge, root/child persistence, committed-response readback, and InsertSuccess behavior.
Descriptor writes remain outside the resource relationship authorization path for this slice.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs for authorization placement after create-new target resolution and before persistence.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister.cs or the chosen persister boundary for same-batch AUTH1-before-dms.Document insertion.

Review tests proving the persister command composes AUTH1 before dms.Document insertion, aborts without committed create-side artifacts when proposed authorization fails, and does not run committed-response readback after the failure.

description: Implement Slice 3 POST precedence for create-new If-Match and existing-resource POST-as-update staging.
acceptance-criteria: POST create-new with If-Match and unauthorized proposed values returns relationship-not-authorized/403 before exposing the create-new 412 response.
POST create-new with If-Match and authorized proposed values returns the existing upsert ETag mismatch/412 result and inserts no rows.
POST requests that resolve to an existing document run proposed-value relationship authorization before exposing the temporary POST-as-update not-implemented/fail-closed result.

Existing-resource POST with failed proposed authorization returns relationship-not-authorized/403, so unauthorized callers cannot distinguish existing-resource POST from create-new POST by status code.

Existing-resource POST with successful proposed authorization returns the explicit temporary not-implemented result and does not execute stored-value authorization, update authorization, no-op logic, or mutation behavior.
Earlier operation-neutral security-configuration and known-but-not-enabled planning failures retain precedence over request-dependent target work.
steps-to-verify: Review DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor ordering for POST If-Match so create-new 412 is evaluated after proposed authorization when required.
Review repository/executor tests for create-new unauthorized+If-Match, create-new authorized+If-Match, existing-resource unauthorized, and existing-resource authorized staging.

description: Add focused unit coverage for POST proposed-value relationship authorization planning, extraction, orchestration, SQL placement, result mapping, and failure precedence.
acceptance-criteria: Planner tests cover proposed-value normal and inverted EdOrg-only strategies, duplicate strategies, OR ordering, AND subjects, NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired, empty claims, known-but-not-enabled strategies, and security-configuration failures.
SQL/compiler tests prove proposed authorization SQL appears before dms.Document insertion in the composed create command.
Extractor/orchestration tests prove missing/null proposed subjects return ProposedValueMissing metadata without composing SQL.
Repository tests prove preflight security-configuration, known-but-not-enabled, and no-claims outcomes happen before reference resolution or target identity lookup.
Write executor tests prove reference-resolution failures and authorization failures remain distinct when no empty-claims short-circuit applies.
Write executor tests prove existing-resource POST-as-update staging is hidden by failed proposed authorization and exposed only after proposed authorization succeeds.
Write executor tests prove create-new If-Match precedence for unauthorized and authorized proposed values.
Profile-aware unit coverage proves proposed authorization reads finalized row-buffer values derived from ProfileAppliedWriteRequest.WritableRequestBody.
steps-to-verify: Review new and updated NUnit tests for Given_ class naming, Setup arrange/act style, It_ test names, FluentAssertions assertions, and FakeItEasy fakes where needed.

description: Add PostgreSQL backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only POST create-new proposed-value authorization.
acceptance-criteria: Authorized POST create-new for a resource using RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly inserts dms.Document and expected root/resource rows.

Unauthorized POST create-new returns 403 and inserts no dms.Document row, no resource root row, no child rows, no dms.ReferentialIdentity row for the attempted identity, no dms.DocumentChangeEvent/update-tracking row, and no dms.Document row carrying an ownership stamp for the attempted resource.
Reference-resolution failure returns the existing reference-resolution response and does not masquerade as authorization denial when no empty-claims short-circuit applies.
Authorization failure after successful reference resolution still prevents all create-side artifacts.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted proves proposed-value Source/Target hierarchy filtering is swapped.
Create-new If-Match with failed proposed authorization returns 403 and inserts no rows.
Create-new If-Match with successful proposed authorization returns 412 and inserts no rows.
Provider assertions do not require global sequences or identity generators to avoid advancing.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration for focused POST relationship authorization tests and fixture setup.

description: Add SQL Server backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only POST create-new proposed-value authorization.
acceptance-criteria: Authorized POST create-new for a resource using RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly inserts dms.Document and expected root/resource rows.

Unauthorized POST create-new returns 403 and inserts no dms.Document row, no resource root row, no child rows, no dms.ReferentialIdentity row for the attempted identity, no dms.DocumentChangeEvent/update-tracking row, and no dms.Document row carrying an ownership stamp for the attempted resource.
Reference-resolution failure returns the existing reference-resolution response and does not masquerade as authorization denial when no empty-claims short-circuit applies.
Authorization failure after successful reference resolution still prevents all create-side artifacts.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted proves proposed-value Source/Target hierarchy filtering is swapped.
SQL Server scalar claim-EdOrg binding below 2000 IDs and dms.BigIntTable TVP binding at 2000 or more IDs remain compatible with proposed authorization checks.
Create-new If-Match with failed proposed authorization returns 403 and inserts no rows.
Create-new If-Match with successful proposed authorization returns 412 and inserts no rows.
Provider assertions do not require global sequences or identity generators to avoid advancing.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration for focused POST relationship authorization tests and fixture setup.

description: Add focused DMS E2E coverage with real claim-set/token wiring for EdOrg-only POST create-new authorization on the relational backend.
acceptance-criteria: A @relational-backend scenario creates a resource through RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly when the caller has an authorized EdOrg claim.
A @relational-backend scenario denies POST create-new with 403 when the caller lacks the required EdOrg relationship.
The denied scenario uses the transitional Slice 3 relationship authorization response shape and avoids asserting final Slice 6 ProblemDetails wording.
The E2E data setup creates only relationship and EdOrg hierarchy data required for the focused POST create-new scenarios.
The E2E suite is placed under src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E and does not use the Instance Management E2E harness.
The feature tags are compatible with the existing relational backend CI tagging/sharding conventions.
steps-to-verify: From src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E, run pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1 and then pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1 before the local E2E run.
If the E2E run fails before API requests, inspect dms-local docker logs for environment/runtime issues before changing test expectations.

description: Run the focused Slice 3 verification set and format changed C# files after implementation.
acceptance-criteria: All changed C# files under src/dms are formatted with csharpier.
Backend unit, plans unit, PostgreSQL integration, SQL Server integration, and focused E2E tests for Slice 3 pass or have documented environmental blockers.
Existing Slice 2 GET-by-id and DELETE relationship authorization tests continue to pass.
Existing write-path smoke tests for descriptors, profile writes, create/update ETag behavior, reference resolution, and guarded no-op continue to pass.
tasks.json remains valid JSON and completed values are updated only for tasks actually completed by the implementation pass.
steps-to-verify: Run the focused @relational-backend E2E scenario added for Slice 3 after rebuilding the dms-local stack.

description: Move relational POST create authorization through the backend-planned authorization lane so repository preflight owns inverted EdOrg-only, empty-claim, and known-but-not-enabled POST precedence.
acceptance-criteria: ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware treats relational POST create requests as backend-planned authorization requests for Slice 3, so RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted is not rejected by the legacy DMS-1056 staged 501 path before reaching the repository.

ProvideAuthorizationFiltersMiddleware preserves raw authorization strategy names with empty filters for relational POST requests, matching the relational GET/DELETE behavior, and does not resolve legacy filter providers or throw the legacy no-EducationOrganization-claims authorization exception.

UpsertHandler continues to pass RelationalAuthorizationContext from ClientAuthorizations and raw AuthorizationStrategyEvaluators to the repository, so repository POST preflight remains the owner of security-configuration, known-but-not-enabled, no-claims, and supported EdOrg-only planning outcomes before reference resolution or target lookup.

Core unit tests cover relational POST with RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted, relational POST with empty EducationOrganization claims, and relational POST with supported EdOrg plus a known-but-not-enabled strategy reaching the backend-planned lane without middleware-shaped 501 or legacy 403 responses.
Existing non-relational authorization middleware behavior and relational GET/DELETE backend-planned behavior remain unchanged.
A focused relational-backend E2E scenario proves POST create-new succeeds through RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted, or through an OR set where inverted is the authorizing strategy.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Middleware/ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware.cs and src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Middleware/ProvideAuthorizationFiltersMiddleware.cs for relational POST inclusion in the backend-planned authorization lane.
From src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E, run pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1 and then pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1 before the local E2E run.

description: Make proposed-value relationship authorization extraction OR-aware so incomplete strategies do not deny POST create when another complete EdOrg-only strategy can authorize.
acceptance-criteria: A missing/null proposed subject in one relationship strategy does not short-circuit evaluation of other OR strategies that have all required proposed values.
POST create succeeds and persists normally when at least one complete proposed EdOrg-only strategy authorizes, even if another configured strategy has proposed element-required metadata.

When no OR strategy authorizes, the relationship failure preserves proposed element-required metadata for incomplete strategies and no-relationship metadata for complete-but-unauthorized strategies in configured-strategy order.

When every configured strategy is incomplete because required proposed values are missing/null, the operation still returns relationship-not-authorized/403 before persistence and does not execute create-side inserts.
The fix preserves multiple subjects within one strategy as AND semantics and multiple strategies as OR semantics for both normal and inverted EdOrg-only strategies.
No proposed authorization SQL parameter is left without a deterministic runtime value or explicit SQL null binding, and token EdOrg IDs remain parameterized.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor.cs for OR-aware handling of incomplete strategies instead of whole-plan short-circuiting on the first missing proposed value.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister.cs and proposed authorization SQL tests for null/missing proposed parameter handling that preserves OR semantics.

description: Add explicit direct EdOrg claim-match metadata to relationship authorization plans for EdOrg-to-EdOrg hierarchy checks.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationAuthObject or RelationshipAuthorizationCheckSpec carries an explicit direct-claim-match flag for auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId checks.
RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner sets the direct-claim-match flag for both stored-value and proposed-value EdOrg hierarchy checks.
Normal and inverted RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly checks keep their existing SubjectValueColumn and ClaimEducationOrganizationIdColumn direction while also allowing direct match.
The flag is not set for any non-EdOrg relationship, Namespace, Ownership, custom-view, or people authorization check.

PageDocumentIdQueryModels exposes direct-match capability for page authorization subjects or strategies, and PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter preserves it when adapting stored relationship authorization for GET-many page SQL.
Existing RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper output and Core.External RelationshipAuthorizationFailure DTOs remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationContracts.cs for the direct-claim-match plan metadata.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.cs for stored and proposed EdOrg hierarchy check construction.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/PageDocumentIdQueryModels.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter.cs for propagation into page authorization models.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RelationshipAuthorizationPlannerTests.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapterTests.cs for assertions that the flag is set only for EdOrg hierarchy checks and is preserved for GET-many page specs.

description: Emit direct EdOrg claim match in single-record relationship authorization SQL for stored and proposed EdOrg hierarchy subjects.
acceptance-criteria: SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler emits direct claim match OR hierarchy EXISTS for EdOrg hierarchy checks whose plan metadata allows direct match.
Stored-value authorization for GET-by-id and DELETE authorizes when the stored subject EdOrgId is directly present in the caller's EdOrg claims without requiring an auth hierarchy row.
Proposed-value authorization for POST create authorizes when the proposed subject EdOrgId is directly present in the caller's EdOrg claims without requiring an auth hierarchy row.
Null stored values still fail with StoredValueNull and null or missing proposed values still fail with ProposedValueMissing before any direct-match success is possible.
Normal hierarchy checks still compare token claims to SourceEducationOrganizationId and subject values to TargetEducationOrganizationId when direct match fails.
Inverted hierarchy checks still compare token claims to TargetEducationOrganizationId and subject values to SourceEducationOrganizationId when direct match fails.
Direct-match SQL is emitted only for checks whose plan metadata allows direct claim match; hierarchy-only checks keep their existing EXISTS behavior.
PostgreSQL array, SQL Server scalar, and SQL Server structured TVP claim parameterization all work for the direct-match predicate without duplicating token claim parameters.
Empty EdOrg claims still use the existing NoClaims planning path and do not reach generated single-record SQL.
Multiple subjects remain AND semantics, multiple strategies remain OR semantics, and duplicate strategies remain distinct failure branches.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler.cs for direct-match SQL in stored and proposed subject failure predicates.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayloadTests.cs or adjacent SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server direct-match assertions.

description: Emit direct EdOrg claim match in GET-many page authorization SQL for stored EdOrg hierarchy subjects.
acceptance-criteria: PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler emits a page predicate equivalent to root EdOrg column direct claim match OR auth hierarchy lookup for RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.
PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler emits the same direct claim match for RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted while preserving the inverted hierarchy-table direction for the fallback lookup.
The direct-match predicate is included in both page DocumentId SQL and TotalCount SQL.
PostgreSQL array, SQL Server scalar, and SQL Server structured TVP claim parameterization all work for GET-many direct match.
Multiple EdOrg subjects within one strategy remain AND semantics and multiple relationship strategies remain OR semantics.
Duplicate supported relationship strategies remain distinct OR branches.
Empty EdOrg claims still use the existing NoClaims planning path and do not reach generated page-query SQL.
Direct-match page SQL is emitted only for authorization subjects or strategies whose page authorization model allows direct claim match.
No page-query direct match is emitted when authorization is absent or when no relationship authorization strategies are present.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/PageDocumentIdQueryModels.cs for direct-match authorization model fields.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler.cs for direct-match SQL in AppendAuthorizationSubjectSql.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/PageDocumentIdSqlCompilerTests.cs for normal, inverted, duplicate, total-count, PostgreSQL, SQL Server scalar, and SQL Server TVP assertions.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests.cs for GET-many captured SQL assertions that include direct match.

description: Add PostgreSQL integration coverage proving direct EdOrg claim match authorizes relationship checks without a pre-existing auth hierarchy row.
acceptance-criteria: POST create with a proposed EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim succeeds when auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId has no matching row for that value.

POST create with a proposed EdOrg subject different from the caller's EdOrg claim still returns relationship-not-authorized/403 and creates no document, root, child, referential identity, or document-change rows.
GET-by-id and DELETE of existing rows with a stored EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim succeed when the auth hierarchy row is absent.
GET-many returns an existing row with a root EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim when the auth hierarchy row is absent.
The generated PostgreSQL authorization command text contains the direct claim-match predicate and still places AUTH1 authorization before dms.Document insertion on POST create.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalPostAuthorizationTests.cs for direct-match POST create coverage.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalGetByIdAuthorizationTests.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalDeleteAuthorizationTests.cs for stored single-record direct-match coverage.

description: Add SQL Server integration coverage proving direct EdOrg claim match authorizes relationship checks without a pre-existing auth hierarchy row.
acceptance-criteria: POST create with a proposed EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim succeeds when auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId has no matching row for that value.

POST create with a proposed EdOrg subject different from the caller's EdOrg claim still returns relationship-not-authorized/403 and creates no document, root, child, referential identity, or document-change rows.
GET-by-id and DELETE of existing rows with a stored EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim succeed when the auth hierarchy row is absent.
GET-many returns an existing row with a root EdOrg subject equal to the caller's EdOrg claim when the auth hierarchy row is absent.
SQL Server scalar claim parameters below the TVP threshold and dms.BigIntTable TVP parameters at or above the threshold both work for direct-match authorization.
The generated SQL Server authorization command text contains the direct claim-match predicate and still places AUTH1 authorization before dms.Document insertion on POST create.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalPostAuthorizationTests.cs for direct-match POST create coverage.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalGetByIdAuthorizationTests.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalDeleteAuthorizationTests.cs for stored single-record direct-match coverage.

description: Add focused relational E2E coverage for LocalEducationAgency create authorization by direct EdOrg claim match.
acceptance-criteria: A @relational-backend scenario with EdOrg claim 255901 POSTs LocalEducationAgency 255901 and receives 201 without pre-seeding an auth hierarchy self tuple for 255901.
A @relational-backend scenario with EdOrg claim 999999 POSTs LocalEducationAgency 255901 and receives 403.
A @relational-backend scenario with empty EdOrg claims POSTs LocalEducationAgency 255901 and receives the existing no-claims authorization failure shape for the current slice.
The authorized scenario does not create LocalEducationAgency 255901 or an auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId self tuple for 255901 during setup.
The focused scenarios use normal RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly claim-set wiring and do not switch setup writes to NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired to bypass relationship authorization.
Existing legacy RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly E2E scenarios remain semantically unchanged except for relational backend and shard tags.
Successful LocalEducationAgency create is followed by a read path assertion proving the created item is accessible through relational authorization.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly.feature and any focused relational companion feature for direct-match LEA scenarios.
From src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E, run pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1 and then pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1 before the local E2E run.
If the E2E run fails before API requests, inspect dms-local docker logs for environment/runtime issues before changing test expectations.

description: Run focused verification for the direct EdOrg claim-match implementation and keep repository artifacts valid.
acceptance-criteria: All changed C# files under src/dms are formatted with csharpier.
tasks.json remains valid JSON with the new direct-match tasks left as completed false until their implementation is actually finished.
Plans unit tests covering RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner, SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler, PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler, and PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter pass.
Backend unit tests covering relational GET-many, GET-by-id, DELETE, and POST authorization orchestration pass.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server relational authorization integration tests pass or have documented environmental blockers.
Focused relational-backend E2E coverage for RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly LocalEducationAgency create passes or has documented environmental blockers.
steps-to-verify: From src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E, run pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1, pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1, and then dotnet test EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.csproj --filter "Category=relational-backend&FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly"
